design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore dungeons, solve puzzles, defeat enemies and bosses, interact with Addams Family members, manage inventory items to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (health management via items) balanced against puzzle-solving and enemy encounters in dungeon exploration
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Uncle Fester on a rescue mission to save baby Pubert, using the dark comedic tone of the Addams Family franchise to contextualize grotesque environments and monster encounters
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Zelda-inspired action-RPG design applied to the Addams Family IP; originally featured selectable difficulty (Easy/Normal/Hard) and control schemes in development, suggesting designers intended to accommodate different player skill levels but removed these in final release, indicating a shift toward a single intended experience

difficulty.continues_system: Password system for progression instead of battery-backed saves
difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based save system (no mid-dungeon checkpoints documented)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Removed in final build: development version included three difficulty settings (Easy/Normal/Hard) and three control method options, but these were deleted before release with no external reference to them, suggesting developer intent to remove player difficulty choice

progression.upgrade_system: Item-based progression: collect equipment and consumable items (potions, etc.) to gain new abilities and overcome obstacles; inventory management required
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story flags and item acquisition unlock access to new dungeon areas; NPC dialogue from Addams Family members gates progression
replayability_hooks: Multiple control methods were offered in development (removed in final)
Side quests documented as present but details not provided
Hidden debug features (level select, sound test) suggest content diversity

world_building_techniques: Cutscenes advancing plot (debug viewer lists 16 cutscene sequences including 'Front gate', 'Mansion', 'Petrified wilderness', 'Swamp', 'Congratulations! Ending')
NPC dialogue with family members providing exposition and gating progression
Environmental storytelling through dungeon design contextualizing Addams Family locations
Manual instruction with story elements (instruction manual documented prototype mechanics and narrative context)

boss_design.philosophy: Bosses presented as encounters within narrative context (final boss preceded by NPC Morobe offering items); puzzle-like elements integrated (item use suggested via dialogue and mechanics, as evidenced by unused Black Egg mechanic intended to trap player)
notable_bosses: Final boss (Pubert's kidnapper context; preceded by trap mechanic with Morobe)

faction_indicators: Addams Family members as allies/NPCs providing aid and exposition
Kidnapper/antagonist faction (Pubert's abductor driving narrative conflict)
danger_signals: Enemy encounters in themed dungeons (Petrified Wilderness, Swamp, Sewage areas)
Resource depletion (health management via limited potion items)
Boss encounters as climactic dungeon challenges
environmental_storytelling: Addams Mansion as hub location connecting narrative and gameplay
Thematic dungeon environments (Petrified Wilderness, Swamp, Foul Sewage, Bookcase passages) reflecting horror-comedy tone
Hidden staircase and environmental puzzle integration (Smashed pillar, Bookcase mechanics)
Gargoyle and vine-climbing sequences suggesting gothic architecture and environmental traversal

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Addams Family Values (SNES) - debugging features, unused items, removed options, anti-piracy, prototype information
wikipedia - Addams Family Values (video game) - release dates, developer credits, gameplay description, reception scores
